thread: Who is directing the grain-plague network behind the village watchers?

evidence.clue: The tainted grain is being monitored by a live village-level watcher rather than left to chance.
Crossbar and Millbrook Spur show active cleanup behavior: false normalcy, blank books, and records stripped before the party arrives.
Jaina's chapter6 field model requires alchemy, necromancy, supply discipline, and years of work rather than opportunistic sabotage.
The party now knows someone arranged road watchers, cleaned depots, and removed at least one body from Millbrook Spur before daylight.
Andorhal's granary district contains at least eight hidden cultists, two local leaders, and an off-ledger treatment building running under ordinary labor cover.
The network can coordinate magical treatment, hidden district workers, rear-lane deliveries, and compartmentalized silence inside a still-functioning city.

readerKnows: The network is now visibly cellular. It has local labor cover, leaders, treatment sites, rear-lane logistics, and the ability to hide inside a working city's routine.
povCharacterKnows: Jaina and Sylvanas know the local Andorhal cell's shape and can hit it tonight. They still do not know the inner circle above those local leaders.
notes: Chapter7 turns this from abstract organization into countable enemy structure without naming the true architect yet.
